Türkçe

Goldenhorn (Neighborhood)

Balat, located on the shores of the Golden Horn in the Fatih district of Istanbul, promises a journey through time with the location of our school. This neighbourhood, which fascinates its visitors with its colourful houses, narrow streets, historical buildings and warm atmosphere, has been increasing its popularity in recent years.

Historical Discovery in the Early Morning:

The ideal spot to start the day by breathing in the historical texture of Balat is

Fener Greek High School

. This historical school, which attracts attention with its red bricks and imposing structure, is one of the important symbols that make up the silhouette of Balat. Afterwards, you can visit the nearby

Sveti Stefan Bulgarian Church (Iron Church)

. Admired for its unique iron structure, this church is an impressive synthesis of gothic and Byzantine architecture.

Getting Lost in the Colourful Streets:

The real magic of Balat begins with getting lost in the narrow streets lined with colourful houses. The bay window houses on

Kiremit Street, Merdivenli Slope

and

Vodina Street

are a paradise for photography enthusiasts. As you walk through these streets, where you will encounter a different colour at every corner, you can trace the traces of the past and soak up the unique atmosphere of the neighbourhood.

Lunch Break: Flavour Stops

When you get tired, you can take a break in Balat’s cosy cafes and restaurants. You can taste freshly baked bagels and pastries from the

historical bakeries

you will come across in the side streets of the neighbourhood, relax in nostalgic coffees or try traditional Turkish and international cuisine in

stylish restaurants

. You can find many different options especially on

Vodina Street

.

Balat has started to host

many art galleries, workshops and antique shops

in recent years. You can come across interesting artefacts and objects in these places while strolling through the narrow streets of the neighbourhood. Especially the shops on

Yıldırım Street

are among the stops not to be missed for antique enthusiasts.

Afternoon Pleasure and View:

At the end of the day, you can go to

Pierre Loti

Hill for a pleasant break against the Golden Horn view. From this hill, which you can easily reach by cable car, you can sip your tea or coffee accompanied by the colourful houses of Balat and the fascinating view of the Golden Horn.

Getting to Balat:

Getting to Balat is quite easy. Since the location of our campus is in the centre of Balat, you will be in this magical atmosphere when you leave the school.

Tips:

– Choose

comfortable shoes

to walk comfortably in the narrow streets of Balat.

– Don’t forget to take water with you, the temperature can be high especially in summer.

– You can immortalise the unique beauties of Balat by taking your camera with you.

– Balat can be quite crowded on weekends, you can choose weekdays for a quieter trip.

– Do not hesitate to chat with the locals, they can give you interesting information about the neighbourhood.

Balat is a hidden paradise of Istanbul waiting to be discovered. This neighbourhood, which will fascinate you with its historical texture, colourful atmosphere and friendly people, will allow you to collect unforgettable memories! Enjoy your discoveries in advance!
